# Sonner

Displays transient toast feedback without interrupting the current task.

## Contents

- [Installation](#installation)
- [Configure Inertia flash types](#configure-inertia-flash-types)
- [Mount the toaster](#mount-the-toaster)
- [Usage](#usage)
- [Types and meaning](#types-and-meaning)
- [Actions and duration](#actions-and-duration)
- [Accessibility and live regions](#accessibility-and-live-regions)

## Installation

Add Sonner from the configured StackTrace registry.

```shell
npx shadcn-vue@latest add @stacktrace/sonner
```

## Configure Inertia flash types

Sonner listens for the `toast` Inertia flash event through `useFlash`. Add its payload to your application's global `InertiaConfig` declaration so TypeScript can infer the toast title, content, and variant. Without this augmentation, the flash event is typed as an empty object.

Merge this interface into your existing `resources/js/Types/globals.d.ts` declaration.

*resources/js/Types/globals.d.ts*

```ts
declare module '@inertiajs/core' {
  export interface InertiaConfig {
    flashDataType: {
      toast?: {
        title: string
        content: string | null
        variant: 'default' | 'destructive'
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## Mount the toaster

Render one `Toaster` near the application root so toasts can be triggered from any frontend component. The component imports the vue-sonner styles, applies theme variables, supplies status icons, and forwards vue-sonner's Toaster props.

```vue
<template>
  <AppShell>
    <slot />
    <Toaster position="bottom-right" />
  </AppShell>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { Toaster } from '@/Components/Sonner'
</script>
```

## Usage

Import `toast` from `vue-sonner` and call it only after an outcome or state change that benefits from brief confirmation. Use a short title, put supporting detail in `description`, and avoid repeating text already visible on the page.

**Toast example**

```vue
<template>
  <div>
    <Toaster />
    <Button variant="outline" @click="toast('Package published successfully')">
      Show toast
    </Button>
  </div>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { toast } from 'vue-sonner'
import { Button } from '@/Components/Button'
import { Toaster } from '@/Components/Sonner'
</script>
```

## Types and meaning

Use `toast.success` for completed work, `toast.error` for a failed operation, `toast.warning` for a condition that needs caution, and `toast.info` for neutral information. Use `toast.loading` only while work is active, then replace or dismiss that same toast when the work settles.

```ts
toast.success('Project published')
toast.error('Project could not be published', {
  description: 'Check your connection and try again.',
})
```

### Promise state

`toast.promise` keeps loading, success, and error feedback in one toast. Use it when those messages all refer to the same operation; keep inline form errors near their fields even if a summary toast is also shown.

```ts
toast.promise(publishProject(), {
  loading: 'Publishing project…',
  success: 'Project published',
  error: 'Project could not be published',
})
```

## Actions and duration

A toast may include a concise optional action such as Undo. Do not put a required decision, destructive confirmation, or the only recovery path in content that disappears automatically. Use `close-button` when users should be able to dismiss persistent or longer messages, and keep the close button's accessible label meaningful for the current language.

## Accessibility and live regions

vue-sonner exposes the toast area as a polite live region. Write text that makes sense when announced without visual context, and avoid triggering several toasts for one operation. Do not move keyboard focus to a toast just because it appeared.

> **Use Alert for persistent or critical feedback: [Alert](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/alert)**
>
> Validation errors, blocking failures, and messages the user must read or act on belong in the page. Use Alert or field-level feedback instead of relying on a transient toast.

Hosted documentation: [Sonner](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/sonner)
